input_fields.label: Product / Service
input_fields.placeholder: e.g. Annual SaaS subscription at $1,200/year, 1:1 business coaching at $3,000/month, custom website design at $5,000
input_fields.hint: Be specific about what you are selling and its core outcome. Include the price tier if relevant.

input_fields.label: Customer Type
input_fields.placeholder: e.g. Marketing manager at a 50-person SaaS company, solo e-commerce founder, enterprise procurement at a Fortune 500
input_fields.hint: Who is the buyer? Their role and company context help tailor the tone and argument.

input_fields.label: Customer's Last Message or Objection
input_fields.placeholder: Paste their exact message here. e.g. 'It looks interesting but $1,200 is a lot. We're already using a free tool. Can you do anything on price?' — or — 'I need to think about it' — or — 'Let me run it by my boss first'
input_fields.hint: The more exact their words, the more targeted the response. Paste their actual message if possible.

input_fields.label: Your Price / Pricing Structure
input_fields.placeholder: e.g. $1,200/year ($100/month) — or — $3,000 one-time — or — $500/month, minimum 3 months
input_fields.hint: Include all pricing tiers if you have them. This helps build the anchoring and discount strategy.

options.label: Close today — get them to say yes right now
Book a call — get them on the phone to close
Get a reply — they have gone silent
Handle the objection — overcome their specific pushback
Send a proposal — design a formal offer
Upsell — move existing customer to higher plan or add-on
Prevent churn — stop a customer from cancelling
Confident & Direct — assumptive, bold, built for decisive buyers (recommended)
Warm & Consultative — empathetic, trust-first, built for relationship-driven sales
Logical & ROI-Led — data and numbers, built for analytical or procurement buyers
Urgency & Scarcity — genuine reason to act now, built for fence-sitters

input_fields.label: Deal Context (Optional)
input_fields.placeholder: e.g. We have been talking for 3 weeks. They tried a free trial. They loved the demo but went quiet after I sent the proposal. This is my second follow-up.
input_fields.hint: How long has the deal been going? What has already been said? Prior objections raised? This adds precision to the output.

input_fields.label: What Does the Customer Care About Most? (Optional)
input_fields.placeholder: e.g. Saving time, reducing cost, looking good to their boss, ease of implementation, proven ROI
input_fields.hint: If you know their primary motivation, the closing argument will be tailored to it.

input_fields.label: Competitor They Mentioned (Optional)
input_fields.placeholder: e.g. They mentioned they are also looking at HubSpot, or currently using a free tool like Notion
input_fields.hint: If they are comparing you to someone, add it here so the output handles that comparison directly.
